method_atom: A diagrammatic/topological calculus that represents a commuting family of algebraic operators (whose symmetric functions generate the centre of an algebra) as geometric tangle-like elements, and exhibits a single manifestly-invariant diagram whose image is provably central. A homomorphism is constructed geometrically from a diagram algebra into the centres of a whole indexed family of algebras, with a distinguished diagram element that is independent of the index and maps to the m-th power sum of the operators. Generating-function and symmetric-function techniques are then used to express images of further diagram elements as functions of the operators and to derive relations among the diagrams.
object_atom: The Murphy operators in the type-A Hecke algebra H_n, the centre of these algebras, and the Homfly skein of the annulus C for which a natural basis is identified.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the geometric-diagram representation of a commuting central operator family plus generating-function/symmetric-function machinery for their power sums and relations; the domain-specific part is the Hecke algebra, its Murphy operators, and the Homfly skein of the annulus.
